description_long: This vehicle features a futuristic, robust design with a central cylindrical body flanked by two large, circular jet intakes and two prominent side-mounted engines. The front is dominated by a large intake or nozzle, with landing gear consisting of two rugged wheels and a lattice support structure. The upper section includes a raised, dome-like cockpit area with visible paneling and a small window, while the rear tapers into a pointed tail with additional mechanical details. The exterior is primarily metallic gray with yellow and black hazard striping on the engine mounts, and various mechanical and electronic components are visible throughout. The overall construction suggests a high-performance, possibly amphibious or hover-capable jet ski intended for rapid urban or waterway travel. The design is both industrial and advanced, with clear attention to aerodynamic and functional details.
description: A futuristic, metallic jet ski with large jet intakes, side engines, and rugged landing gear, featuring a raised cockpit and hazard-striped details.
description_view_0: Front view shows the large central intake, two circular jet intakes, and the rugged landing gear with lattice support.
description_view_1: Side view highlights the elongated body, side-mounted engines, cockpit dome, and the pointed tail section.
description_view_2: Rear view reveals the tapered tail, engine exhausts, and the symmetrical arrangement of the jet intakes and engines.
description_view_3: Opposite side view displays the same structural features as view 1, with clear visibility of the cockpit and engine mounts.
attribute_handlebar_design: The handlebar is centrally mounted, appears tubular with dark grips, and includes visible control elements and possibly a display or control panel at the center.
attribute_seat_configuration: The seat is a single, elongated structure positioned behind the cockpit dome, contoured for one or possibly two riders, and finished in a matching gray material.
